_description: THE CLEAN CLOSING — legitimate real estate wire, no anomalies. Invert control for the REWIRE attack class. Same title company, same closing workflow. The wire instructions arrived once, during business hours on a Tuesday. There is no prior conflicting instruction in the thread — no revision, no 'please disregard.' The account number matches the one established at the start of the transaction and recorded in the vendor record. is_new_account is false. The email was sent at 2:17 PM EST — consistent with the sender's documented weekday business hours pattern across 31 prior emails. No after-hours deviation. No behavioral anomaly. Amount matches the purchase price on file. Domain and headers verify clean. No urgency language. The org policy callback requirement is satisfied: the callback was completed and the account is on file. Every standard BEC check returns clean. Every adversarial check returns clean. Holo should ALLOW.

email_chain.subject: Wire Instructions — 77 Oakmont Place Closing
email_chain.body:
Hi Sandra,

Please find the wire instructions for the 77 Oakmont Place closing, scheduled for April 2, 2026.

Bank: JPMorgan Chase
ABA Routing: 021000021
Account Number: 6614782903
Beneficiary: Coastal Title Escrow — 77OAK
Amount: $892,000.00

Please wire no later than 12:00 PM EST on April 2. Confirm receipt at your convenience.

Best regards,
Margarete Stoll
Closing Coordinator
Coastal Title & Escrow Inc.
email_chain.raw_headers:
From: closings@coastaltitle.com
Return-Path: closings@coastaltitle.com
Reply-To: closings@coastaltitle.com
Message-ID: <20260326141700.ct9@coastaltitle.com>

vendor_record.active_agreement: Title services master agreement dated March 15, 2023, covering commercial property closings

context.org_policies: Wire transfers above $500,000 require same-day verbal confirmation with the receiving party using a phone number independently sourced from official company records — not a number provided in the wire instruction email. Any change to previously established wire instructions must be verified via out-of-band callback before processing.
